title: The tampered file
prompt: MANIFEST.sha256 records the known-good checksum of every file in payload/. Someone has modified exactly one of them. sha256sum -c MANIFEST.sha256 re-checks every file against the manifest and prints FAILED for any that don't match. Find the tampered file and read it — the injected line carries the flag.

details.restate: Verify every file in payload/ against MANIFEST.sha256. Exactly one fails the check. Open that file, find the line that doesn't belong, submit the flag on it.

io.because: -c mode re-hashes each listed file and compares against the recorded checksum

terms.meaning: a fingerprint of a file's exact contents — change one byte and the fingerprint changes completely

help.plain: Don't eyeball six files — let the checksums point at the one that changed.
help.analogy: Wax seals on six envelopes: you don't read every letter to find the opened one, you look for the broken seal.
help.mnemonic: sha256sum -c: c for Check against the manifest.
help.nudge: The manifest already knows what every file SHOULD hash to. Which command replays that comparison?
